purpose: Bestiary for cyber. Each entry feeds the fauna-spawner, the loot tables, and the bestiary lens. Cyber's 'creatures' are mostly engineered — drones, ice constructs, augmented predators — with a thin layer of feral adaptations.

creatures.behavior_pattern: patrol — fixed routes with override-on-anomaly. Cluster fire when threat exceeds threshold. Disengage if a corp_certified ID-token is presented (the failsafe — and the lever every decker exploits).
creatures.lore: The default lethality unit of corp_tier. Mass-produced by Sentinel-Yamada under perpetual contract with Grid Authority. The MK-7 model has been in service for 23 years with only cosmetic updates — corps refuse to fund a replacement because the failsafe is too useful.

creatures.behavior_pattern: lurk in protected sub-systems; fire when intrusion detected; pursue retreating signals to source if Black-class. White-class merely freezes the intruder. Black-class fries the wetware.
creatures.lore: Three classes: White (defensive — locks you out), Gray (active — alerts corp security), Black (lethal — physically destroys the decker's brain via neural backlash). Black ICE is illegal in 8 of the 9 worlds; cyber Grid Authority taxes it 14% and pretends it doesn't exist.

creatures.behavior_pattern: passive — a stuck fragment of a deceased decker's neural patterns, looping in residual server memory. Will broadcast their last living minute on repeat to any compatible neural interface within 50m. Some pay credits to hunt them; some pay credits to put them to rest.
creatures.lore: Born from the late-stage decker mortality problem: when a decker dies wired in, their neural patterns sometimes persist in the server fabric for years. The Static Collective runs a 'rest service' that catalogues and erases them with consent from the family.

creatures.behavior_pattern: swarm — feed on discarded chrome (the metal, not the augs). Hunt the rats and you'll find embedded scraps — recycled augs that fell off a corp-class chrome shop's waste-pickup. Aggressive at night.
creatures.lore: An emergent species. The original ratus norvegicus population developed metallic-resistance over 80 generations of feeding from chrome shop waste. They now have visible silver hairs that conduct electricity — they sometimes short-circuit each other in skirmishes.

creatures.behavior_pattern: hunter — solo or pair. Overclock-augmented humans (or post-humans) who hunt for chrome, organs, or contract bounties. Lethal at close range; vulnerable to EMP. Carry their own combat ice and back-door payloads.
creatures.lore: The cyber equivalent of mythic-tier predator. A combat-augmented decker who survived past 35 typically transitions to aug_predator full-time — the chrome inside them is worth more in pieces than they earn legally. Cited mortality: 28 months from full conversion.
creatures.cross_world_corollary: superhero: rogue_avatar. fantasy: drake. crime: contract_killer.

creatures.behavior_pattern: ambush-on-trespass. Designed as a final-failsafe by corp ice_engineers. Triggers if any of 7 nested authentication layers fail. Kills the trespasser via neural backlash if their interface is still connected; physically dispatches a sec-team if not.
creatures.lore: Every corp has one but officially none of them do. Killing one in combat is the surest way to earn an aug_predator contract on yourself within 18 hours. The Static Collective claims to have killed 11 over the past decade; corps have confirmed 0.
